commit.message:
Unify all agents on state-interim, add anti-contamination ethics and Responder guidance

Retrospective fixes from Flight.htb engagement:

1. All agents now use state-interim MCP (previously only discovery agents).
   Technique agents can write critical mid-run discoveries (captured hashes,
   credentials) so the orchestrator sees them immediately via event watcher
   instead of waiting for agent return.

2. Added "do not crack" enforcement to all agent templates — agents save
   hashes via add_credential() and continue; orchestrator routes cracking
   to credential-cracking-agent in parallel.

3. Added Target Knowledge Ethics to all 11 agent templates — agents may
   use general pentest methodology from any source but must not use specific
   knowledge of the current target (e.g., HTB writeups for the box being tested).

4. Added Responder for NTLM Capture section to web-exploit-agent with
   explicit paths, flags, and daemon monitoring guidance (check logs via
   docker exec, not send_command/read_output).

5. Fixed deserialization-java skill's weak "Route to hash cracking" →
   "Route to **credential-cracking**".

6. Updated all docs (agents, architecture, engagement-state, mcp-servers,
   skills-reference, writing-skills) and state-server README/docstrings
   to reflect unified state-interim access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix monthly A/X to compare full W1 week vs full prior month last week

Monthly timeframe: each AMDX phase is a WEEK, not a day. The A/X
classification should compare W1's full weekly candle (high/low)
against the prior month's last full weekly candle — NOT Monday vs
Friday within those weeks. Monday vs Friday is correct for the
weekly timeframe where each phase is a day. Also fixed pending
check to flag W1 as incomplete when it's the current week and
market is still open on any weekday.

commit.message:
v0.3.0: Single source of truth architecture + 2-command structure

state.json is now the single source of truth for all project state.
plan.md, KANBAN.md, board.html, and HANDOFF.md are derived views,
regenerated from state.json — never edited directly.

Key changes:
- state.json carries full plan hierarchy (Phase → Milestone → Sprint → Task)
- Tiered loading: session start reads ~100 lines, full plan on-demand
- Collapse roadmap.md + milestones.md + execution-plan.md into plan.md
- Remove /ship-decide — decisions detected and captured proactively
- 2 commands: /ship-init, /ship-go. Everything else is automatic.
- Backlog lives in state.json (3 tiers: main, exploration, tech-debt)
- Deterministic traceability: task → sprint → milestone → phase
- Add CLAUDE.md with core principles
- Update all references to use state.json instead of separate files

Switch Claude to --output-format stream-json for real-time token streaming

--print buffers the entire response before outputting anything (explains 2+ min blank terminal).
stream-json outputs one JSONL event per line as tokens are generated — text appears immediately.
Server now parses stream-json events and forwards text blocks to the SSE terminal in real time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Route Google OAuth through scholarblitz.com via PKCE flow

Instead of Supabase's managed OAuth (which shows supabase.co on Google's
account chooser), initiate a direct PKCE flow from /api/auth/google so
Google shows "scholarblitz.com" to users.

- Add /api/auth/google route: generates PKCE code_verifier/challenge,
  stores in httpOnly cookies, redirects to Google with redirect_uri=scholarblitz.com/callback
- Update /callback route: detects direct Google flow via state cookie,
  exchanges code server-side using client_secret, then signs in via
  supabase.auth.signInWithIdToken
- Update login/signup to navigate to /api/auth/google instead of Supabase OAuth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: quality_metricsモジュール追加 — 品質トレンドの統計的可視化

AI生成コードの増加に対し、品質ゲートが形骸化していないかを

commit.message:
feat: N-user support, Extra Fair scoring, niche/mainstream vibe, multi-cuisine

- Generalize /api/suggest to accept users[] (N users, legacy user_a/b still works)
- Simple mode: N-user fairness = max(times) + 0.3 * stdDev(times)
- Extra Fair mode: weighted multi-criteria scoring (distance, cuisine, price, vibe)
  - cuisine: multi-select, any matching type scores 1
  - vibe: niche (few reviews) vs mainstream (many reviews) via log-normalized userRatingCount
  - global score = mean(utilities) * (1 - 0.3 * stdDev) — penalizes unfairness
- Return top 10 candidates for client-side swipe phase (was 3)
- Fetch userRatingCount from Places API for vibe scoring
- Add centroid + maxPairwiseRadius helpers for N-user midpoint/radius
- Update scoring test for new stdDev formula

commit.message:
perf(discoveries): filter by community BEFORE batch neighbor computation

The _compute_discoveries function was computing neighbors for ALL 700+
unreviewed identities before filtering by community. For Fox Family with
~100 identities, this caused 30+ second server timeouts. Now filters
unreviewed and confirmed lists by community_identity_ids before the
expensive batch_best_neighbor_distances call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add real FITS data optimizations: adaptive seeing, tight linking, FP reduction

Key changes for processing real Pan-STARRS FITS images:

- Measure field seeing from bright source PSFs instead of using fixed
  FWHM thresholds (0.8-1.2 arcsec). Adaptive range: 0.5x-2.5x measured
  seeing. Critical for varying atmospheric conditions across nights.

- Add tight linking mode for real data: smaller prediction radius
  (min(8, max(5, speed*0.20))), stricter velocity tolerance (12%),
  and tighter magnitude matching (1.5 mag) reduce chance alignments.

- Add magnitude-aware stationary source rejection: nearest-neighbor
  check at 7px radius rejects sources whose closest cross-frame match
  has both similar position AND similar brightness (within 0.5 mag).
  Uses single nearest neighbor, not any-within-radius, to avoid false
  rejections in sparse fields.

- Add bright star linkage detection in criterion 9: objects brighter
  than mag 18.5 in real data are catalogued stars, not undiscovered
  asteroids. Eliminates high-confidence FPs from coincidental star
  alignments.

- Add linear motion (criterion 5) as hard gate alongside persistence
  and false positive rejection.

- Performance: defer PSF fitting to validation step (only measure
  tracklet candidates, not all thousands of sources). Use find_objects()
  for efficient source bounding boxes.

Results on real IASC campaign data (7 Sep 2024, Pan-STARRS 2):
- Synthetic validation: 3/3 detected, 0 FP (unchanged)
- Negative field (XY75_p11): 8 FP candidates (down from 462 baseline)
- Positive field (XY75_p00): Real asteroid detected at 80% confidence

finding usst/justfile which lacks private recipes. Must use _self (which
includes --justfile and --working-directory) everywhere.

Affected: default, _validate-all, list-users, fresh-build

- run_pipeline: 수집→정제→청킹→임베딩→적재 통합 실행, 단계별 오류 복구 (T-115)
- 테스트: 43개 추가 → 전체 144개, 커버리지 96%

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(numerology): add complete Chaldean Numerology UI — 76 service endpoints + 92 raw calculators

Implements the full Chaldean Numerology frontend for Grahvani:

- Types: 594-line type definitions covering all 168 API endpoints
- API layer: direct astro-engine calls with typed service + raw methods
- Constants: data-driven metadata for 76 services across 10 categories
- Navigation: header dropdown + mobile nav + sidebar config
- Layout: ChaldeanLayout with horizontal category pills (mirrors VedicLayout)
- Forms: 7 reusable input components (base, two-person, family, team, custom fields)
- Results: pattern-detecting renderer with 12+ specialized visualizers
  - ServiceResultLayout: single-card orchestrator with HR dividers
  - DetailedAnalysisAccordion: smart section grid (scores, timelines, profiles, colors)
- Pages: overview dashboard + 10 category pages + raw calculator explorer
- Mutation hooks: generic useChaldeanService + useChaldeanRaw

Verified: tsc --noEmit (0 errors), next build (success, all routes compiled)

commit.message:
fix: all 7 ShipSafer extensions compile clean + ShipSafer branding icons

- Fixed TypeScript errors across all 7 extensions (zero tsc errors):
  - shipsafer-agent: unused `token` param → `_token`
  - shipsafer-ai: `placeholder` → `placeHolder`, unused `token` → `_token`
  - shipsafer-cloud: `contextValue` needs `override`, `placeholder` → `placeHolder`
  - shipsafer-db: removed unused `execAsync`, `contextValue` override, `placeholder` → `placeHolder` (×2)
  - shipsafer-monitoring: `contextValue` override, removed unused `loading` field,
    `org`/`project` params → `_org`/`_project`, drop `private readonly` on unused `_sentryConfig`,
    `placeholder` → `placeHolder`
  - shipsafer-rest: unused params → `_extensionUri`/`_webview`, `placeholder` → `placeHolder`
  - shipsafer-terminal-ai: `onDidWriteTerminalData` cast to `any`, `placeholder` → `placeHolder` (×2)
- Updated tsconfig.base.json: `skipLibCheck: true`, `types: ["node"]` (resolves vscode.d.ts conflict)
- Removed conflicting `typeRoots` from all 7 extension tsconfigs
- Deployed ShipSafer branding icons:
  - resources/darwin/code.icns (from SVG via rsvg-convert + iconutil)
  - resources/linux/code.png (256×256 PNG)
  - resources/win32/code.ico (multi-size ICO via Pillow)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: case-insensitive SKU matching and improved campaign empty state

- Replace case-sensitive Array.includes() with a lowercase Set for SKU matching
  so PH API SKUs match Firestore items regardless of casing
- Add [Render] console log showing matched item count for easier debugging
- Distinguish 'No Recommended Items' vs 'No Items Available' empty state
  when campaign SKUs are active but no menu items match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
